summary: McKenzie solicits reader input on what statistics they'd like to see him track and share publicly following the launch of the web version of Bingo Card Creator. He outlines plans to upgrade his internal stats tracking and lists several metrics he's considering, including signups, logins, customer lifecycles, cohort analysis, and popular activities.
key_points: The web version of Bingo Card Creator enables richer data collection than the desktop version
McKenzie practices radical transparency by sharing business metrics publicly
He plans to track customer lifecycle metrics including time-to-purchase and retention
He recognizes the value of cohort analysis for understanding user behavior patterns
money_quotes: I'm going to spend a portion of my weekend upgrading my internal stats tracking, which means "more stuff to share with you guys".
key_insight: Transitioning from desktop to web software unlocks deeper analytics capabilities around customer lifecycles and behavior that drive better business decisions.
